system.description:
A roaring mob of Ork Boyz, charging forward in a green tide of violence. Each Boy is a hulking brute armed with crude but deadly weapons, and together they form an overwhelming mass of muscle, tusks, and fury.

Magnitude: 30

Armour: Flak armour (Body 2)

Gear: Shiny bitz, really small Squigs

Source: Deathwatch Core Rulebook, p. 366

system.benefit: Use Primitive and SP Basic weapons without penalty
system.description: The character can use Primitive and Solid Projectile Basic class weapons without penalty.
The character can fire heavy weapons using Semi-Auto Burst or Full Auto Burst without bracing, and he does not suffer the –30 penalty for failing to brace.
The character adds +2 to damage inflicted in melee.

system.benefit: Extra attack on All Out Attack hit
system.description: If the character successfully hits his target using the All Out Attack Action, he may spend his Reaction to make an additional attack using the same bonuses or penalties as the original attack.

system.benefit: Recover damage as if Lightly Damaged
system.description: When undergoing medical treatment or healing from injuries, the character recovers Damage as if Lightly Damaged.

system.benefit: Toughness Test to ignore Stunned effects
system.description: If ever Stunned, a successful Toughness Test allows the character to ignore the effects.

system.benefit: Use Chain and Primitive Melee weapons without penalty
system.description: The character can use Chain and Primitive Melee class weapons without penalty.

system.benefit: Use Primitive and SP Pistol weapons without penalty
system.description: The character can use Primitive and Solid Projectile Pistol class weapons without penalty.

system.benefit: +2 Damage when dealing Critical Damage with unarmed or knife
system.description: When dealing Critical Damage with an unarmed attack or knife, the character deals an additional +2 points of Damage.
Whenever the character suffers Critical Damage, halve the result (rounding up).
A creature with this trait deals an extra 3 points of damage when it charges in the same round. A horde with this trait does an additional 1d10 damage when it charges in the same round.

Unreliable Ork weapons are not Unreliable in an Ork’s hands. Ork technology defies the laws of physics and engineering, functioning through a combination of brute force and the latent psychic field generated by the Ork race. Ork weapons and equipment, while crude and unreliable in the hands of other races, work just fine for Orks. An Ork never suffers penalties for using Ork-made equipment.

All Orks are latently psychic, an ability that increases in strength the more of them there are in one place. This bolsters their confidence and courage to near-fearless levels when they gather en masse. For every additional Ork within 10m, the Ork’s Willpower gains a +10 bonus to resist the effects of Fear and Pinning. In a Horde, Orks are immune to Fear and Pinning.
Sturdy creatures are hard to move and thus gain a +20 bonus to tests made to resist grappling and uses of the Takedown Talent.

The creature's Toughness Characteristic is unnatural. Each time this trait is gained, double its Toughness Bonus. For example, a creature with Toughness 41 normally has a 4 Toughness Bonus. With this trait, its Toughness Bonus increases to 8.

The trait may be gained multiple times. Each time you apply this trait to Toughness, the Bonus multiplier increases by 1. For example, one selection multiplies the Toughness Bonus by ×2, two selections by ×3, and three selections ×4. Note this trait does not modify the creature's movement.

During Opposed Characteristic Tests, on a success, the bonus multiplier is added to the degree of success. Any additional multipliers for Unnatural Characteristics simply improve the existing multiplier by 1.

When taking a Skill Test based upon Toughness with the Unnatural trait, the base Difficulty of the Test is staged downwards one level for degree of multiplier past normal (to a maximum of +30).

During Opposed Characteristic Tests, where success is achieved, the Unnatural multiplier is added to the degrees of success. In the case of a tie, the participant without the Unnatural Characteristic trait loses the Opposed Test.

The creatures that make up the horde are capable of swarming over enemies and ripping them apart with a thousand cuts. The horde with this trait always rolls an extra 1d10 for damage caused by the horde in close combat as long as it has a magnitude equal to or greater than 20.
A crude, loud, slug-firing pistol favoured by Ork Boyz.
A loud, fully automatic rifle used by Ork Boyz.
A brutal, heavy-bladed melee weapon wielded by Ork Boyz.
